title.zh: 卷十二 王劉張李彭盧列傳
title.en: Volume 12: Biographies of Wang, Liu, Zhang, Li, Peng, Lu
title.raw: 卷十二 王劉張李彭盧列傳 Volume 12: Biographies of Wang, Liu, Zhang, Li, Peng, Lu

sentences.zh: 王昌一名郎，趙國邯鄲人也。

translations.literal: Wang Chang, another name Lang, was a man of Handan in the state of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Chang, who also went by the name Wang Lang, came from Handan in Zhao.

sentences.zh: 素為卜相工，明星曆，常以為河北有天子氣。

translations.literal: He had long worked as a diviner and physiognomist, understood the stars and calendars, and constantly believed that north of the Yellow River there was the aura of a Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: He made his living reading omens and faces, knew the stars and the calendar, and had long been convinced that the region north of the river bore the portents of an emperor.

sentences.zh: 時趙繆王子林好奇數，任俠於趙、魏間，多通豪猾，而郎與之親善。

translations.literal: At that time Liu Lin, a son of Prince Miao of Zhao, was fond of occult numerology, acted the knight-errant between Zhao and Wei, associated much with powerful and crafty men, and Lang was intimate with him.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Lin, a son of Zhao’s Prince Miao, dabbled in prognostication and swaggered as a bravo between Zhao and Wei, cultivating local strongmen; Wang Lang was close to him.

sentences.zh: 初，王莽篡位，長安中或自稱成帝子子輿者，莽殺之。

translations.literal: Earlier, when Wang Mang seized the throne, someone in Chang’an who called himself Ziyu, a son of Emperor Cheng, was killed by Mang.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang usurped the throne, a man in Chang’an had claimed to be Ziyu, a son of Emperor Cheng; Mang had him executed.

sentences.zh: 郎緣是詐稱真子輿，云『母故成帝謳者，嘗下殿卒僵，須臾有黃氣從上下，半日乃解，遂妊身就館。

translations.literal: Lang on this account falsely called himself the true Ziyu, saying: ‘My mother had been a singer for Emperor Cheng; once when descending the hall she suddenly collapsed stiff; in a moment yellow vapour came from above and below; only after half a day did she recover, and then she was pregnant and went to the lying-in quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang therefore passed himself off as the genuine Liu Ziyu, claiming that his mother had once been a palace singer to Emperor Cheng, that she had fainted on the steps in a fit from which yellow mist seemed to pour for half a day before she stirred, and that she then conceived and was sent to the birth house.

sentences.zh: 趙後欲害之，偽易他人子，以故得全。

translations.literal: Empress Zhao wished to harm him; she falsely exchanged another person’s child; by this means he was preserved whole.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Zhao tried to destroy the infant, swapped in another baby to deceive the court, and so the true child—so he said—was spared.

sentences.zh: 子輿年十二，識命者郎中李曼卿，與俱至蜀；

translations.literal: When Ziyu was twelve, he met the fate-reader Gentleman-of-the-Palace Li Manqing and together with him went to Shu.
translations.idiomatic: At twelve, the boy supposedly met the physiognomist Li Manqing, an imperial courtier, and traveled with him into Shu.

sentences.zh: 十七，到丹陽；

translations.literal: At seventeen, he arrived at Danyang.
translations.idiomatic: At seventeen he reached Danyang.

sentences.zh: 二十，還長安；

translations.literal: At twenty, he returned to Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: At twenty he was back in Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 展轉中山，來往燕、趙，以須天時』。

translations.literal: He shifted to Zhongshan, coming and going between Yan and Zhao, in order to await the heavenly season.’
translations.idiomatic: He drifted through Zhongshan and between Yan and Zhao, waiting for the moment Heaven would favour him.’

sentences.zh: 林等愈動疑惑，乃與趙國大豪李育、張參等通謀，規共立郎。

translations.literal: Lin and the others were increasingly moved to doubt, and then with great magnates of Zhao such as Li Yu and Zhang Can secretly plotted, planning together to establish Lang.
translations.idiomatic: The story unsettled Liu Lin and his circle; they conspired with Zhao notables Li Yu and Zhang Can to set Wang Lang on the throne.

sentences.zh: 會人間傳赤眉將度河，林等因此宣言赤眉當至，立劉子輿以觀眾心，百姓多信之。

translations.literal: It happened that among the people it was rumored the Red Eyebrows would cross the river; Lin and the others on this account proclaimed that the Red Eyebrows were about to arrive, and that they would establish Liu Ziyu in order to observe the hearts of the multitude; the common people for the most part believed it.
translations.idiomatic: When word spread that the Red Eyebrows were about to cross the Yellow River, Liu Lin’s faction declared that the dynasty’s heir, Liu Ziyu, must be enthroned to meet the crisis—and the credulous populace largely believed them.

sentences.zh: 更始元年十二月，林等遂率車騎數百，晨入邯鄲城，止於王宮，立郎為天子。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month of the first year of Gengshi, Lin and the others then led several hundred chariots and horsemen, entered Handan city at dawn, stopped at the royal palace, and established Lang as Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month of Gengshi 1 (24 CE), they rode into Handan at dawn with hundreds of mounted troops, seized the old Zhao palace, and proclaimed Wang Lang emperor.

sentences.zh: 林為丞相，李育為大司馬，張參為大將軍。

translations.literal: Lin became Chancellor, Li Yu became Grand Marshal, Zhang Can became Grand General.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Lin took the title of chancellor, Li Yu grand marshal, and Zhang Can commander-in-chief.

sentences.zh: 分遣將帥，徇下幽、冀。

translations.literal: They separately dispatched generals to bring You and Ji to submission.
translations.idiomatic: They sent columns of generals to overrun Youzhou and Jizhou.

sentences.zh: 移檄州郡曰：『制詔部刺史、郡太守：朕，孝成皇帝子子輿者也。

translations.literal: They moved a proclamation to provinces and commanderies, saying: ‘Imperial order to regional inspectors and grand administrators: I am Ziyu, son of Emperor Xiaocheng.
translations.idiomatic: A circular went out to every province: ‘By imperial rescript to the regional inspectors and grand administrators: I am Liu Ziyu, son of Emperor Cheng.’

sentences.zh: 昔遭趙氏之禍，因以王莽篡殺，賴知命者將護朕躬，解形河濱，削跡趙、魏。

translations.literal: Formerly I suffered the calamity of the Zhao clan; because of it Wang Mang usurped and killed; relying on one who knew fate who led and protected my person, I released my form by the river's bank and erased my traces in Zhao and Wei.
translations.idiomatic: ‘When the Zhao faction brought disaster upon me, Wang Mang seized the throne and tried to destroy me; a sage who read Heaven’s will shielded me, I vanished by the riverbanks, and I lived nameless between Zhao and Wei.’

sentences.zh: 王莽竊位，獲罪於天，天命佑漢，故使東郡太守翟義、嚴鄉侯劉信，擁兵征討，出入胡、漢。

translations.literal: Wang Mang stole the position, incurred guilt from Heaven; Heaven's mandate favoured Han; therefore it caused Grand Administrator of Dong Commandery Zhai Yi and Marquis of Yanxiang Liu Xin to raise troops and campaign, going in and out among the Hu and the Han.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Wang Mang’s usurpation outraged Heaven, yet Heaven still favoured Han—so it stirred Zhai Yi of Dong commandery and Marquis Liu Xin of Yanxiang to raise armies that marched even among the northern tribes.’

sentences.zh: 普天率土，知朕隱在人間。

translations.literal: Throughout heaven and along the earth, they knew I was hidden among mankind.
translations.idiomatic: ‘All under Heaven knew I was alive, though hidden among common men.’

sentences.zh: 南嶽諸劉，為其先驅。

translations.literal: The various Lius of the southern marchmount served as their vanguard.
translations.idiomatic: ‘The Liu clans of the southern marchmount rode before them as vanguard.’

sentences.zh: 朕仰觀天文，乃興於斯，以今月壬辰即位趙宮。

translations.literal: I looked up at the patterns of heaven and thereby rose here; on this month's renchen day I ascended the throne in the Zhao palace.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Reading the stars, I knew the hour had come; on the renchen day of this month I took the throne in the palace of Zhao.’

sentences.zh: 休氣熏蒸，應時獲雨。

translations.literal: Auspicious vapours steamed richly; in response to the season rain was obtained.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Auspicious mists rose thickly, and timely rain fell at once.’

sentences.zh: 蓋聞為國，子之襲父，古今不易。

translations.literal: I have heard that in governing a state, the son inheriting the father does not change from ancient to modern.
translations.idiomatic: ‘It is an unchanging law of statecraft that a son succeeds his father.’

sentences.zh: 劉聖公未知朕，故且持帝號。

translations.literal: Liu Shenggong does not yet know me; therefore for now he holds the imperial title.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Liu Xuan has not yet learned of me, so he still bears the imperial style—for the moment.’

sentences.zh: 諸興義兵，咸以助朕，皆當裂土享祚子孫。

translations.literal: Those who have raised righteous armies, all to assist me, shall all enfeoff land and enjoy blessing for descendants.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Every man who took up arms for the righteous cause to aid me shall receive a fief and a blessing to pass to his sons.’

sentences.zh: 已詔聖公及翟太守，亟與功臣詣行在所。

translations.literal: I have already ordered Shenggong and Grand Administrator Zhai urgently to come with meritorious ministers to the travelling headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: ‘I have summoned Liu Xuan and Prefect Zhai to bring the chief ministers to my camp without delay.’

sentences.zh: 疑刺史、二千石皆聖公所置，未睹朕之沈滯，或不識去就，強者負力，弱者惶惑。

translations.literal: I suspect the regional inspectors and two-thousand-bushel ranks are all appointed by Shenggong, have not seen my submerged delay, or do not know whether to go or stay; the strong rely on force, the weak are anxious and confused.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Your inspectors and salary-ranked officials were Liu Xuan’s appointments; they have not seen how long I lay in hiding, and some waver—the bold cling to force, the timid are lost.’

sentences.zh: 今元元創痍，已過半矣，朕甚悼焉，故遣使者班下詔書。

translations.literal: Now the black-haired people are wounded, already more than half gone; I deeply mourn them; therefore I dispatch envoys to distribute the edict downward.
translations.idiomatic: ‘The common people are more than half ruined by war; I grieve for them, and send envoys to publish this edict in every quarter.’

sentences.zh: 』郎以百姓思漢，既多言翟義不死，故祚稱之，以從人望。

translations.literal: ’ Lang because the hundred surnames longed for Han, and moreover many said Zhai Yi did not die, therefore falsely called himself that, in order to follow people's hopes.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Wang Lang knew the people still yearned for Han and that many believed Zhai Yi had never died; he traded on that name to win their allegiance.

sentences.zh: 於是趙國以北，遼東以西，皆從風而靡。

translations.literal: Thereupon north of the state of Zhao and west of Liaodong, all followed the wind and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: North of Zhao and west of Liaodong, county after county bent to his proclamation.

sentences.zh: 明年，光武自薊得郎檄，南走信都，發兵徇帝縣，遂攻柏人，不下。

translations.literal: The next year, Guangwu from Ji obtained Lang's proclamation, fled south to Xindu, raised troops and brought imperial counties to submission, then attacked Bairen, but did not take it.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Guangwu, fleeing from Ji, intercepted Wang Lang’s circular, raced south to Xindu, mobilized the region, and struck Bairen—but could not break the wall.

sentences.zh: 議者以為守柏人不如定巨鹿，光武乃引兵東北圍巨鹿。

translations.literal: Those who deliberated thought guarding Bairen was not as good as securing Julu; Guangwu then led troops northeast to besiege Julu.
translations.idiomatic: His advisers argued that a stalemate at Bairen was worse than securing Julu, so he swung northeast and laid siege to Julu.

sentences.zh: 郎太守王饒據城，數十日連攻不克。

translations.literal: Lang's grand administrator Wang Rao held the city; for several tens of days continuous attack did not overcome it.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Rao, Wang Lang’s prefect, held the city fast; weeks of assaults failed to crack it.

sentences.zh: 耿純說曰：『久守王饒，士眾疲敝，不如及大兵精銳，進攻邯鄲。

translations.literal: Geng Chun urged, saying: ‘Long guarding Wang Rao, soldiers and multitude are worn; it is not as good as while the great army is keen-edged, to advance and attack Handan.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Chun urged him: ‘If we linger against Wang Rao our troops will waste away; strike Handan now while the army is still sharp.’

sentences.zh: 若王郎已誅，王饒不戰自服矣。

translations.literal: If Wang Lang is already executed, Wang Rao without battle will submit of himself.
translations.idiomatic: Kill Wang Lang, and Wang Rao will yield without another blow.’

sentences.zh: 』光武善其計，乃留將軍鄧滿守巨鹿，而進軍邯鄲，屯其郭北門。

translations.literal: ’ Guangwu approved his plan, then left General Deng Man to guard Julu, while advancing the army to Handan and encamping at the outer wall's north gate.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Guangwu agreed, left General Deng Man to mask Julu, and marched on Handan, camping outside the north gate of the suburbs.

sentences.zh: 郎數出戰不利，乃使其諫議大夫杜威持節請降。

translations.literal: Lang repeatedly went out to battle without success, then sent his Counsellor-in-chief Du Wei bearing credentials to request surrender.
translations.idiomatic: After repeated defeats in the field, Wang Lang sent Du Wei, his counsellor of remonstrance, with imperial insignia to sue for peace.

sentences.zh: 威雅稱郎實成帝遺體。

translations.literal: Wei elegantly claimed Lang was truly the bodily legacy of Emperor Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: Du Wei insisted, with elaborate courtesy, that Wang Lang was in truth Emperor Cheng’s surviving son.

sentences.zh: 光武曰：『設使成帝復生，天下不可得，況詐子輿者乎！

translations.literal: Guangwu said: ‘Suppose Emperor Cheng were reborn; the realm under heaven could not be obtained; how much less one who falsely calls himself Ziyu!
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu replied: ‘If Emperor Cheng himself rose from the grave he could not have the empire back—let alone a swindler calling himself Ziyu!’

sentences.zh: 』威請求萬戶侯。

translations.literal: ’ Wei requested to obtain a marquis of ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Du Wei asked for a marquisate of ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 光武曰：『顧得全身可矣。

translations.literal: ’ Guangwu said: ‘I only wish to obtain preservation of the whole body; that is enough.
translations.idiomatic: ’ ‘I will settle for his life,’ said Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 』威曰：『邯鄲雖鄙，並力固守，尚曠日月，終不君臣相率但全身而已。

translations.literal: ’ Wei said: ‘Handan though mean, if we combine strength and firmly guard, still will waste months and days; in the end ruler and ministers will not lead one another to only preserve the whole body.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Du Wei answered: ‘Handan may be a provincial town, but united we can hold it for months; ruler and ministers will never simply march out to save their skins.’

sentences.zh: 』遂辭而去。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon he took leave and departed.
translations.idiomatic: ’ With that he withdrew.

sentences.zh: 因急攻之，二十餘日，郎少傅李立為反間，開門內漢兵，遂拔邯鄲。

translations.literal: Thereupon they urgently attacked it; after more than twenty days, Lang's Junior Tutor Li Li acted as double agent, opened the gate and admitted Han troops, and thereupon they took Handan.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu pressed the siege; after three weeks Li Li, Wang Lang’s junior tutor, turned traitor, opened the gates to the Han army, and Handan fell.

sentences.zh: 郎夜亡走，道死，追斬之。

translations.literal: Lang by night fled; on the road he died; they pursued and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lang fled by night, died on the road, and his pursuers struck off his head.

sentences.zh: 劉永者，梁郡睢陽人，梁孝王八世孫也。

translations.literal: Liu Yong was a man of Suiyang in Liang commandery, an eighth-generation descendant of King Xiao of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong came from Suiyang in Liang commandery, the eighth generation in descent from Prince Xiao of Liang.

sentences.zh: 傳國至父立。

translations.literal: The transmission of the state reached his father Li.
translations.idiomatic: The principality had passed down to his father, Liu Li.

sentences.zh: 元始中，立與平帝外家衛氏交通，為王莽所誅。

translations.literal: In the Yuanshi period, Li had intercourse with the Wei clan, the maternal relatives of Emperor Ping, and was executed by Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: During Yuanshi (1–5 CE), Liu Li had dealings with the Wei family, Emperor Ping’s in-laws, and Wang Mang had him executed.

sentences.zh: 建武二年夏，光武遣虎牙大將軍蓋延等伐永。

translations.literal: In summer of the second year of Jianwu, Guangwu dispatched Grand General with Fangs of the Tiger Gai Yan and others to campaign against Yong.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of Jianwu 2 (26 CE), Guangwu sent Gai Yan, grand general “Tiger’s Fangs,” and others against Liu Yong.

sentences.zh: 初，陳留人蘇茂為更始討難將軍，與朱鮪等守洛陽。

translations.literal: Earlier, Su Mao of Chenliu had been General Who Subdues Difficulties for Gengshi, and with Zhu Wei and others guarded Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao of Chenliu had served the Gengshi court as “general who quells rebellion” and, with Zhu Wei, held Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 鮪既降漢，茂亦歸命，光武因使茂與蓋延俱攻永。

translations.literal: When Wei had already surrendered to Han, Mao also submitted to the command; Guangwu thereupon ordered Mao together with Gai Yan jointly to attack Yong.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhu Wei came over to Han, Su Mao followed suit; Guangwu paired him with Gai Yan for the drive on Liu Yong.

sentences.zh: 軍中不相能，茂遂反，殺淮陽太守，掠得數縣。

translations.literal: In the army they did not get along; Mao thereupon rebelled, killed the grand administrator of Huaiyang, and plundered and obtained several counties.
translations.idiomatic: The two generals quarreled in camp; Su Mao mutinied, slew the prefect of Huaiyang, and seized several counties.

sentences.zh: 據廣樂而臣於永。

translations.literal: He occupied Guangyue and became minister to Yong.
translations.idiomatic: He fortified Guangyue and submitted to Liu Yong.

sentences.zh: 永以茂為大司馬、淮陽王。

translations.literal: Yong appointed Mao Grand Marshal and King of Huaiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong named him grand marshal and king of Huaiyang.

sentences.zh: 蓋延遂圍睢陽，數月，拔之，永將家屬走虞。

translations.literal: Gai Yan thereupon besieged Suiyang; after several months he took it; Yong led family dependents and fled to Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan besieged Suiyang for months, took it, and Liu Yong fled with his household to Yu.

sentences.zh: 虞人反，殺其母及妻子，永與麾下數十人奔譙。

translations.literal: The people of Yu rebelled, killed his mother and wife and children; Yong with several tens of men under his banner fled to Qiao.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Yu rose against him, murdered his mother, wife, and children, and he escaped to Qiao with only a few dozen followers.

sentences.zh: 蘇茂。

translations.literal: Su Mao.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao.

sentences.zh: 佼彊、周建合軍救永，為蓋延所敗，茂奔還廣樂，彊、建從永走保湖陵。

translations.literal: Jiao Qiang and Zhou Jian combined armies to rescue Yong, were defeated by Gai Yan; Mao fled back to Guangyue; Qiang and Jian followed Yong in flight to protect Huling.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao Qiang and Zhou Jian marched to relieve him but were routed by Gai Yan; Su Mao fell back to Guangyue while Jiao and Zhou shepherded Liu Yong to the refuge of Huling.

sentences.zh: 三年春，永遣使立張步為齊王，董憲為海西王。

translations.literal: In spring of the third year, Yong dispatched envoys to establish Zhang Bu as King of Qi and Dong Xian as King of Haixi.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 3, Liu Yong sent envoys to invest Zhang Bu as king of Qi and Dong Xian as king of Haixi.

sentences.zh: 於是遣大司馬吳漢等圍蘇茂於廣樂，周建率眾救茂，茂、建戰敗，棄城復還湖陵，而睢陽人反城迎永。

translations.literal: Thereupon he dispatched Grand Marshal Wu Han and others to besiege Su Mao at Guangyue; Zhou Jian led a multitude to rescue Mao; Mao and Jian were defeated in battle, abandoned the city and returned again to Huling, but the people of Suiyang turned the city and welcomed Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu then sent Wu Han, grand marshal, to invest Su Mao at Guangyue; Zhou Jian tried to break the siege but both men were beaten and fled again to Huling—while Suiyang threw open its gates and welcomed Liu Yong back.

sentences.zh: 吳漢與蓋延等合軍圍之，城中食盡，永與茂、建走酂。

translations.literal: Wu Han together with Gai Yan and others combined armies and besieged it; food in the city was exhausted; Yong together with Mao and Jian fled to Zan.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han joined Gai Yan, starved the city, and drove Liu Yong, Su Mao, and Zhou Jian out toward Zan.

sentences.zh: 諸將追急，永將慶吾斬永首降，封吾為列侯。

translations.literal: The generals pressed the pursuit urgently; Yong's general Qing Wu beheaded Yong's head and surrendered; Wu was enfeoffed as a full marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Hard pressed in the retreat, Liu Yong was struck down by his own officer Qing Wu, who brought in the head and received a full marquisate.

sentences.zh: 蘇茂、周建奔垂惠，共立永子紆為梁王。

translations.literal: Su Mao and Zhou Jian fled to Chuihui, together established Yong's son Yu as King of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao and Zhou Jian withdrew to Chuihui and set up Liu Yong’s son Liu Yu as king of Liang.

sentences.zh: 佼彊還保西防。

translations.literal: Jiao Qiang returned to protect Xifang.
translations.idiomatic: Jiao Qiang fell back to the fortress of Xifang.

sentences.zh: 四年秋，遣捕虜將軍馬武、騎都尉王霸圍紆、建於垂惠，蘇茂將五校兵救之，紆、建亦出兵與武等戰，不克，而建兄子誦反，閉城門拒之。

translations.literal: In autumn of the fourth year, he dispatched General Who Captures Foes Ma Wu and Cavalry Commandant Wang Ba to besiege Yu and Jian at Chuihui; Su Mao led Five Colonel troops to rescue them; Yu and Jian also sent troops to battle with Wu and others, did not overcome, but Jian's elder brother's son Song rebelled, closed the city gate and refused them.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Guangwu sent Ma Wu and Wang Ba to besiege Liu Yu and Zhou Jian at Chuihui; Su Mao marched in with the Five Colonels’ host to relieve them, and the defenders sallied against Ma Wu without success—until Zhou Jian’s nephew Zhou Song mutinied and barred the gates against his own side.

sentences.zh: 建、茂、紆等皆走，建於道死，茂奔下邳與董憲合，紆奔佼彊。

translations.literal: Jian, Mao, Yu and others all fled; Jian died on the road; Mao fled to Xiapi and joined with Dong Xian; Yu fled to Jiao Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Jian, Su Mao, and Liu Yu broke and ran; Zhou Jian died on the road, Su Mao joined Dong Xian at Xiapi, and Liu Yu fled to Jiao Qiang.

sentences.zh: 五年，遣驃騎大將軍杜茂攻佼彊於西防，彊與劉紆奔董憲。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, he dispatched Grand General of Agile Cavalry Du Mao to attack Jiao Qiang at Xifang; Qiang together with Liu Yu fled to Dong Xian.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 5 he sent Du Mao, grand general of agile cavalry, against Jiao Qiang at Xifang; Jiao Qiang and Liu Yu took refuge with Dong Xian.

sentences.zh: 時，平狄將軍龐萌反叛，遂襲破蓋延，引兵與董憲連和，自號東平王，屯桃鄉之北。

translations.literal: At that time, General Who Pacifies the Di Pang Meng rebelled, thereupon raided and defeated Gai Yan, led troops and allied with Dong Xian, styled himself King of Dongping, and encamped north of Taoxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Pang Meng, the “pacifier of the Di” general, mutinied, ambushed and routed Gai Yan, joined Dong Xian, proclaimed himself king of Dongping, and camped north of Taoxiang.

sentences.zh: 龐萌，山陽人。

translations.literal: Pang Meng was a man of Shanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Meng was a native of Shanyang.

sentences.zh: 初亡命在下江兵中。

translations.literal: Earlier he was a fugitive among the Lower Jiang troops.
translations.idiomatic: He had first been an outlaw in the Lower Jiang army.

sentences.zh: 更始立，以為冀州牧，將兵屬尚書令謝躬，共破王郎。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he was made shepherd of Ji Province, led troops subordinate to Secretary Director Xie Gong, and together destroyed Wang Lang.
translations.idiomatic: Under the Gengshi regime he became shepherd of Ji, served under Secretary Director Xie Gong, and helped bring down Wang Lang.

sentences.zh: 及躬敗，萌乃歸降，光武即位，以為侍中。

translations.literal: When Gong was defeated, Meng then returned and surrendered; when Guangwu ascended the throne, he was made palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: After Xie Gong’s defeat he came over to Guangwu, who on taking the throne made him a palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 萌為人遜順，甚見信愛。

translations.literal: Meng as a person was yielding and compliant, and was greatly seen as trusted and loved.
translations.idiomatic: He was modest and obliging, and the emperor trusted him deeply.

sentences.zh: 帝常稱曰：『可以托六尺之孤，寄百里之命者，龐萌是也。

translations.literal: The emperor constantly praised, saying: ‘He who can be entrusted with the orphan of six feet, to whom the command of a hundred li may be committed, is Pang Meng.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor used to say, ‘The man to whom one could entrust a boy heir and the fate of a state is Pang Meng.’

sentences.zh: 』拜為平狄將軍，與蓋延共擊董憲。

translations.literal: ’ He was appointed General Who Pacifies the Di, together with Gai Yan jointly to strike Dong Xian.
translations.idiomatic: ’ He was commissioned general “Who Pacifies the Di” and sent with Gai Yan against Dong Xian.

sentences.zh: 時，詔書獨下延而不及萌，萌以為延譖己，自疑，遂反。

translations.literal: At that time the edict went down only to Yan and did not reach Meng; Meng thought Yan had slandered him, doubted himself, and thereupon rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: When an edict named Gai Yan but omitted Pang Meng, he decided Gai Yan had maligned him, brooded, and rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 帝聞之，大怒，乃自將討萌。

translations.literal: When the emperor heard it, he was greatly angry, then personally led to campaign against Meng.
translations.idiomatic: Enraged, Guangwu took the field in person against him.

sentences.zh: 與諸將書曰：『吾常以龐萌社稷之臣，將軍得無笑其言乎？

translations.literal: He wrote to the generals, saying: ‘I always took Pang Meng as a minister of the altars of soil and grain; will the generals not laugh at those words?
translations.idiomatic: He wrote to his commanders: ‘I called Pang Meng a pillar of the state—do you gentlemen find that amusing now?’

sentences.zh: 老賊當族。

translations.literal: The old rebel ought to be exterminated as a clan.
translations.idiomatic: That old traitor shall be extirpated root and branch.’

sentences.zh: 其各厲兵馬，會睢陽！

translations.literal: Each of you sharpen chariots and horses, assemble at Suiyang!
translations.idiomatic: Sharpen your arms and rally at Suiyang!’

sentences.zh: 』憲聞帝自討龐萌，乃與劉紆、蘇茂、佼彊去下邳，還蘭陵，使茂、彊助萌，合兵三萬，急圍桃城。

translations.literal: ’ When Xian heard the emperor personally was campaigning against Pang Meng, then together with Liu Yu, Su Mao, and Jiao Qiang left Xiapi, returned to Lanling, ordered Mao and Qiang to assist Meng, combined armies thirty thousand, and urgently besieged Taocheng.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Dong Xian, hearing that the emperor himself was moving against Pang Meng, quit Xiapi for Lanling with Liu Yu, Su Mao, and Jiao Qiang, sent Su and Jiao to reinforce Pang Meng, and threw thirty thousand men around Taocheng.

sentences.zh: 帝時幸蒙，聞之，乃留輜重，自將輕騎三千，步卒數萬，晨夜馳赴，師次任城，去桃鄉六十里。

translations.literal: The emperor at that time was visiting Meng; when he heard it, he then left baggage trains, personally led three thousand light cavalry and several tens of thousands of foot soldiers, galloped day and night; the army halted at Rencheng, sixty li from Taoxiang.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was at Meng when the news came; he shed his baggage train, took three thousand horse and several ten thousand foot, and rode night and day to Rencheng, sixty li from Taoxiang.

sentences.zh: 旦日，諸將請進，賊亦勒兵挑戰，帝不聽，乃休士養銳，以挫其鋒。

translations.literal: At dawn the generals requested to advance; the bandits also marshalled troops and challenged battle; the emperor did not listen, then rested soldiers and nurtured keenness, in order to blunt their edge.
translations.idiomatic: At daybreak his generals begged to attack while the rebels formed for battle; he refused, rested the men, and let the enemy’s edge dull.

sentences.zh: 城中聞車駕至，眾心益固。

translations.literal: Those in the city heard the imperial carriage had arrived; the multitude's hearts became more firm.
translations.idiomatic: The garrison, learning the emperor had come, took heart and stood firmer.

sentences.zh: 時吳漢等在東郡，馳使召之。

translations.literal: At that time Wu Han and others were in Dong commandery; he galloped envoys to summon them.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han was still in Dong commandery; couriers raced to fetch him.

sentences.zh: 萌等乃悉兵攻城，二十餘日，眾疲困而不能下。

translations.literal: Meng and others then used all troops to attack the city; after more than twenty days the multitude was weary and exhausted but could not take it.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Meng threw his whole force against the walls for three weeks until his army flagged and still could not break in.

sentences.zh: 及吳漢與諸將到，乃率眾軍進桃戰，而帝親自搏戰，大破之。

translations.literal: When Wu Han and the generals arrived, then he led the massed armies to advance to Taoxiang battle, and the emperor personally joined combat, greatly defeating them.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Han and the other generals arrived, Han troops advanced on Taoxiang; the emperor himself entered the fight and shattered the enemy.

sentences.zh: 萌、茂、彊夜棄輜重逃奔，董憲乃與劉紆悉其兵數萬人屯昌慮，自將銳卒拒新陽。

translations.literal: Meng, Mao, and Qiang by night abandoned baggage and fled; Dong Xian then together with Liu Yu used all his troops several tens of thousands and encamped at Changlü, personally led picked soldiers to resist Xinyang.
translations.idiomatic: Pang Meng, Su Mao, and Jiao Qiang fled by night, leaving their trains; Dong Xian gathered tens of thousands with Liu Yu at Changlü and led an elite corps to block Xinyang.

sentences.zh: 帝先遣吳漢擊破之，憲走還昌慮。

translations.literal: The emperor first dispatched Wu Han to strike and defeat him; Xian fled back to Changlü.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu first sent Wu Han, who broke him and drove Dong Xian back to Changlü.

sentences.zh: 漢進守之，憲恐，乃招誘五校餘賊步騎數千人屯建陽，去昌慮三十里。

translations.literal: Han advanced and guarded it; Xian was afraid, then summoned and enticed the remnant bandits of the Five Colonels, several thousand foot and cavalry, to encamp at Jianyang, thirty li from Changlü.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han pressed the siege; Dong Xian, unnerved, lured several thousand surviving Five Colonels bandits to Jianyang, thirty li from Changlü.

sentences.zh: 帝王蕃，去憲所百餘里。

translations.literal: The emperor was at Wangfan, more than a hundred li from Xian's place.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor halted at Wangfan, a little over a hundred li from Dong Xian’s camp.

sentences.zh: 諸將請進，帝不聽，知五校乏食當退，敕各堅壁以待其敝。

translations.literal: The generals requested to advance; the emperor did not listen, knowing the Five Colonels lacked food and would retreat, ordered each to strengthen walls and await their exhaustion.
translations.idiomatic: Again his generals urged attack; he refused, knowing the Five Colonels were short of grain and would withdraw, and ordered every column to hold fast behind its walls until they broke.

sentences.zh: 頃之，五校糧盡，果引去。

translations.literal: Shortly, the Five Colonels' grain was exhausted; they indeed led away.
translations.idiomatic: Soon their supplies gave out and they marched off as he had foreseen.

sentences.zh: 帝乃親臨，四面攻憲，三日，復大破之，眾皆奔散。

translations.literal: The emperor then personally approached, attacked Xian on four sides; in three days, again greatly defeated him; the multitude all fled scattered.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu then came up himself, stormed Dong Xian from every side, and in three days routed him again; his host melted away.

sentences.zh: 遣吳漢追擊之，佼彊將其眾降，蘇茂奔張步，憲及龐萌走入繒山。

translations.literal: He dispatched Wu Han to pursue and strike them; Jiao Qiang led his multitude to surrender; Su Mao fled to Zhang Bu; Xian together with Pang Meng entered Zeng Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Wu Han in pursuit; Jiao Qiang surrendered his command, Su Mao fled to Zhang Bu, and Dong Xian with Pang Meng took to the Zeng hills.

sentences.zh: 數日，吏士聞憲尚在，復往往相聚，得數百騎，迎憲入郯城。

translations.literal: After several days, clerks and soldiers hearing Xian still was alive, again often gathered together, obtained several hundred cavalry, welcomed Xian into Tan city.
translations.idiomatic: Within days, hearing Dong Xian still lived, scattered troops rallied a few hundred horsemen and escorted him into Tan.

sentences.zh: 吳漢等復攻拔郯，憲與龐萌走保朐。

translations.literal: Wu Han and others again attacked and took Tan; Xian together with Pang Meng fled to protect Qu.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han stormed Tan; Dong Xian and Pang Meng fell back to the refuge of Qu.

sentences.zh: 劉紆不知所歸，軍士高扈斬其首降，梁地悉平。

translations.literal: Liu Yu did not know where to return; soldier Gao Hu beheaded his head and surrendered; Liang territory was entirely pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yu wandered without shelter until a trooper named Gao Hu struck off his head and came in; Liang was finally quiet.

sentences.zh: 吳漢進圍朐。

translations.literal: Wu Han advanced and besieged Qu.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han moved on to besiege Qu.

sentences.zh: 明年，城中谷盡，憲、萌潛出，襲取贛榆，瑯邪太守陳俊攻之，憲、萌走澤中。

translations.literal: The next year, grain in the city was exhausted; Xian and Meng secretly went out, raided and took Ganyu; grand administrator of Langye Chen Jun attacked them; Xian and Meng fled into the marshes.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, when the granaries were empty, Dong Xian and Pang Meng slipped out, seized Ganyu by surprise, but Chen Jun, prefect of Langye, counterattacked and drove them into the coastal marshes.

sentences.zh: 會吳漢下朐城，進盡獲其妻子。

translations.literal: It happened that Wu Han took Qu city, advanced and completely captured his wife and children.
translations.idiomatic: Then Wu Han stormed Qu and seized Dong Xian’s wife and children.

sentences.zh: 憲乃流涕謝其將士曰：『妻子皆已得矣。

translations.literal: Xian then wept tears and apologized to his generals and soldiers, saying: ‘Wife and children are already obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Xian wept and said to his men, ‘They have taken my family.’

sentences.zh: 嗟乎！

translations.literal: Alas!
translations.idiomatic: Alas!

sentences.zh: 久苦諸卿。

translations.literal: For long I have bitterly troubled you gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: I have put you all through too much.’

sentences.zh: 』乃將數十騎夜去，欲從間道歸降，而吳漢校尉韓湛追斬憲於方與，方與人黔陵亦斬萌，皆傳首洛陽。

translations.literal: ’ He then led several tens of cavalry and left by night, wishing by a secret path to return and surrender, but Wu Han's commandant Han Zhan pursued and beheaded Xian at Fangyu; a man of Fangyu, Qianling, also beheaded Meng; all sent heads to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: ’ He slipped away at night with a few dozen horsemen, hoping to reach Han lines by a back path, but Han Zhan, a commandant under Wu Han, ran him down at Fangyu and killed him; a local man named Qianling struck off Pang Meng’s head as well, and both heads were sent to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 封韓湛為列侯，黔陵關內侯。

translations.literal: Han Zhan was enfeoffed as a full marquis; Qianling as marquis within the passes.
translations.idiomatic: Han Zhan was made a full marquis and Qianling a marquis-within-the-passes.

sentences.zh: 張步字文公，瑯邪不其人也。

translations.literal: Zhang Bu, courtesy name Wengong, was a man of Buqi in Langye.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu, style Wengong, was a native of Buqi in Langye.

sentences.zh: 漢兵之起，步亦聚眾數千，轉攻傍縣，下數城，自為五威將軍，遂據本郡。

translations.literal: When Han troops rose, Bu also gathered a multitude of several thousand, turned to attack neighbouring counties, took several cities, styled himself General of the Five Powers, and thereupon occupied his native commandery.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han armies rose, he rallied several thousand men, raided neighbouring counties, seized a string of towns, called himself “general of the five powers,” and made himself master of Langye.

sentences.zh: 更始遣魏郡王閎為瑯邪太守，步拒之，不得進。

translations.literal: Gengshi dispatched Wang Hong of Wei commandery as grand administrator of Langye; Bu resisted him and he could not advance.
translations.idiomatic: The Gengshi court sent Wang Hong of Wei to take Langye; Zhang Bu blocked him and he could not push inland.

sentences.zh: 閎為檄，曉喻吏人降，得贛榆等六縣，收兵數千人，與步戰，不勝。

translations.literal: Hong composed a proclamation, clearly instructing clerks and people to surrender; he obtained six counties including Ganyu, gathered troops several thousand, battled with Bu, and did not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong issued a manifesto urging officials and commoners to submit, won six counties including Ganyu, raised several thousand troops, and fought Zhang Bu—but lost.

sentences.zh: 時梁王劉永自以更始所立，貪步兵強，承制拜步輔漢大將軍、忠節侯，督青、徐二州，使征不從命者。

translations.literal: At that time King of Liang Liu Yong, considering himself established by Gengshi, coveted Bu's infantry strength, by imperial commission appointed Bu General-in-Chief Who Aids Han and Marquis of Loyal Integrity, supervised Qing and Xu two provinces, and ordered him to campaign against those who did not obey commands.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong of Liang, counting himself a creature of the Gengshi regime, coveted Zhang Bu’s footsoldiers and, acting under Liu Yong’s commission, named him grand general “who aids Han” and marquis of loyal integrity, put him in charge of Qing and Xu, and told him to subdue all who defied orders.

sentences.zh: 步貪其爵號，遂受之。

translations.literal: Bu coveted those titles and ranks, thereupon accepted them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu wanted the titles and accepted.

sentences.zh: 乃理兵於劇，以弟弘為衛將軍，弘弟藍玄武大將軍，藍弟壽高密太守。

translations.literal: He then ordered troops at Ju, appointed younger brother Hong as General Who Guards the Palace, Hong's younger brother Lan as Grand General of the Black Warrior, Lan's younger brother Shou as grand administrator of Gaomi.
translations.idiomatic: He drilled his army at Ju, made his brother Zhang Hong general of the guard, Zhang Hong’s brother Zhang Lan grand general of the Black Warrior, and Zhang Lan’s brother Zhang Shou prefect of Gaomi.

sentences.zh: 遣將徇太山、東萊、城陽、膠東、北海、濟南、齊諸郡，皆下之。

translations.literal: He dispatched generals to bring to submission Mount Tai, Donglai, Chengyang, Jiaodong, Beihai, Jinan, and Qi commanderies; all were taken.
translations.idiomatic: He sent columns through Taishan, Donglai, Chengyang, Jiaodong, Beihai, Jinan, and Qi, and every one of those commanderies fell to him.

sentences.zh: 步拓地浸廣，兵甲日盛。

translations.literal: Bu's expanded territory gradually grew wide; arms and armour daily flourished.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu’s domain spread and his army grew stronger by the day.

sentences.zh: 王閎懼其眾散，乃詣步相見，欲誘以義方。

translations.literal: Wang Hong feared his multitude would scatter, then went to Bu to meet him, wishing to entice him with righteous norms.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong, fearing his own men would melt away, went in person to see Zhang Bu and tried to win him with moral argument.

sentences.zh: 步大陳兵引閎，怒曰：『步有何過，君前見攻之甚乎！

translations.literal: Bu greatly arrayed troops and led Hong in, angrily saying: ‘What fault has Bu, that you formerly attacked me so severely!
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu drew up his host and had Wang Hong brought before him. ‘What wrong have I done,’ he demanded, ‘that you should have attacked me so fiercely?’

sentences.zh: 』閎按劍曰：『太守奉朝命，而文公擁兵相距，閎攻賊耳，何謂甚邪！

translations.literal: ’ Hong pressed his sword and said: ‘The grand administrator carries court commands, while Wengong holds troops and resists at distance; Hong attacks bandits—what is called severe!
translations.idiomatic: ’ Wang Hong’s hand went to his sword. ‘I hold an imperial commission,’ he said, ‘and you, Wengong, block me with arms—I strike rebels, not friends. Where is the excess?’

sentences.zh: 』步嘿然，良久，離席跪謝，乃陳樂獻酒，待以上賓之禮，令閎關掌郡事。

translations.literal: ’ Bu was silent; after a long while he left the mat, knelt and apologized, then set out music and presented wine, treated him with the rites of a superior guest, and ordered Hong to close gates and manage commandery affairs.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Zhang Bu fell silent, then rose from his seat, knelt, and apologized. He had music played and wine poured, treated Wang Hong as an honoured guest, and put him in charge of the commandery.

sentences.zh: 建武三年，光武遣光祿大夫伏隆持節使齊，拜步為東萊太守。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianwu, Guangwu dispatched Grand Counsellor of the Household Fu Long bearing credentials as envoy to Qi, and appointed Bu as grand administrator of Donglai.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 3 Guangwu sent Fu Long, a grand counsellor of the household, to Qi with credentials to appoint Zhang Bu grand administrator of Donglai.

sentences.zh: 劉永聞隆至劇，乃馳遣立步為齊王，步即殺隆而受永命。

translations.literal: When Liu Yong heard Long had reached Ju, he galloped dispatch to establish Bu as King of Qi; Bu immediately killed Long and accepted Yong's command.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong, learning that Fu Long had reached Ju, rushed messengers to invest Zhang Bu as king of Qi; Zhang Bu murdered Fu Long on the spot and took Liu Yong’s commission instead.

sentences.zh: 是時，帝方北憂漁陽，南事梁、楚，故步得專集齊地，據郡十二，及劉永死，步等欲立永子紆為天子，自為定漢公，置百官。

translations.literal: At this time the emperor was just north anxious about Yuyang, south occupied with Liang and Chu; therefore Bu was able exclusively to gather Qi territory, occupying twelve commanderies; when Liu Yong died, Bu and others wished to establish Yong's son Yu as Son of Heaven, himself to be Duke Who Stabilizes Han, and set up hundred officials.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was tied down in the north at Yuyang and in the south in Liang and Chu, so Zhang Bu had a free hand in Qi—twelve commanderies at his back. After Liu Yong died he and his faction meant to set Liu Yong’s son Liu Yu on the throne, style himself “duke who settles Han,” and appoint a full court.

sentences.zh: 王閎諫曰：『梁王以奉本朝之故，是以山東頗能歸之。

translations.literal: Wang Hong remonstrated, saying: ‘The King of Liang because he served this court's cause, therefore east of the mountains could somewhat submit to him.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong urged him: ‘The people east of the mountains followed the king of Liang because he still paid lip service to the legitimate court.’

sentences.zh: 今尊立其子，將疑眾心。

translations.literal: Now if you honour and establish his son, you will cause the multitude's hearts to doubt.
translations.idiomatic: To exalt his son now will only sow doubt among your followers.’

sentences.zh: 且齊人多詐，宜且詳之。

translations.literal: Moreover Qi people are much deceitful; you ought for the time being to examine it carefully.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, the men of Qi are a slippery lot—think it through carefully.’

sentences.zh: 』步乃止。

translations.literal: ’ Bu then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Zhang Bu dropped the plan.

sentences.zh: 五年，步聞帝將攻之，以其將費邑為濟南王，屯歷下。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, Bu heard the emperor would attack him, appointed his general Fei Yi as King of Jinan, and encamped at Lixia.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 5, hearing that Guangwu was coming against him, he made his general Fei Yi king of Jinan and posted him at Lixia.

sentences.zh: 冬，建威大將軍耿弇破斬費邑，進拔臨淄。

translations.literal: In winter, Grand General Who Establishes Might Geng Yan defeated and beheaded Fei Yi, advanced and took Linzi.
translations.idiomatic: That winter Geng Yan, grand general who “establishes might,” killed Fei Yi and drove on to take Linzi.

sentences.zh: 步以弇兵少遠客，可一舉而取，乃悉將其眾攻弇於臨淄。

translations.literal: Bu thought Yan's troops were few and far guests, could be taken in one stroke, then led all his multitude to attack Yan at Linzi.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu judged Geng Yan’s force small and far from home, thought one blow would finish him, and threw his whole army at Linzi.

sentences.zh: 步兵大敗，還奔劇。

translations.literal: Bu's infantry was greatly defeated, returned fleeing to Ju.
translations.idiomatic: His infantry was shattered and fled back to Ju.

sentences.zh: 帝自幸劇。

translations.literal: The emperor personally visited Ju.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor came in person to Ju.

sentences.zh: 步退保平壽，蘇茂將萬餘人來救之。

translations.literal: Bu retreated to protect Pingshou; Su Mao led more than ten thousand men to rescue him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu fell back to Pingshou; Su Mao marched in with ten thousand men to relieve him.

sentences.zh: 茂讓步曰：『以南陽兵精，延岑善戰，而耿弇走之。

translations.literal: Mao reproached Bu, saying: ‘With Nanyang troops keen and Yan Cen good at battle, yet Geng Yan put them to flight.
translations.idiomatic: Su Mao rebuked him: ‘Nanyang’s host was crack, Yan Cen a seasoned fighter—yet Geng Yan routed them.’

sentences.zh: 大王奈何就攻其營？

translations.literal: Great King, why did you go and attack his camp?
translations.idiomatic: Why, my lord, did you throw yourself straight at his camp?’

sentences.zh: 既呼茂，不能待邪？

translations.literal: Since you had summoned Mao, could you not wait?
translations.idiomatic: You had called for me—could you not have waited?’

sentences.zh: 』步曰：『負負，無可言者。

translations.literal: ’ Bu said: ‘Shame shame; there is nothing to say.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Zhang Bu said only, ‘I was wrong—twice wrong. There is nothing to say.’

sentences.zh: 』帝乃遣使告步、茂，能相斬降者，封為列侯。

translations.literal: ’ The emperor then dispatched envoys to tell Bu and Mao, whoever can mutually behead and surrender will be enfeoffed as a full marquis.
translations.idiomatic: ’ The emperor sent word to Zhang Bu and Su Mao: whoever killed the other and surrendered would receive a full marquisate.

sentences.zh: 步遂斬茂，使使奉其首降。

translations.literal: Bu thereupon beheaded Mao, sent envoys presenting his head to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu cut down Su Mao and sent his head in with an offer of submission.

sentences.zh: 步三弟各自系所在獄，皆赦之。

translations.literal: Bu's three younger brothers each bound themselves in the prisons where they were; all were pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: His three brothers had bound themselves in the local jails; they too were pardoned.

sentences.zh: 封步為安丘侯，後與家屬居洛陽。

translations.literal: Bu was enfeoffed as Marquis of Anqiu; afterward he dwelt at Luoyang with family dependents.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu was ennobled as marquis of Anqiu and later lived in Luoyang with his family.

sentences.zh: 王閎亦詣劇降。

translations.literal: Wang Hong also went to Ju and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong also came to Ju and submitted.

sentences.zh: 八年夏，步將妻子逃奔臨淮，與弟弘、藍欲招其故眾，乘船入海，瑯邪太守陳俊追擊斬之。

translations.literal: In summer of the eighth year, Bu led wife and children and fled to Linhuai, together with younger brothers Hong and Lan wished to summon his former multitude, boarded boats to enter the sea; grand administrator of Langye Chen Jun pursued, struck, and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of Jianwu 8 he fled with his wife and children to Linhuai, joined his brothers Hong and Lan in a scheme to rally his old followers and put to sea, but Chen Jun, prefect of Langye, overtook and killed him.

sentences.zh: 王閎者，王莽叔父平阿侯譚之子也，哀帝時為中常侍。

translations.literal: Wang Hong was the son of Marquis Ping'e Tan, Wang Mang's paternal uncle; in Emperor Ai's time he was a regular palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong was a son of Prince Ping’e Tan, Wang Mang’s uncle by blood; under Emperor Ai he served as a regular palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 時幸臣董賢為大司馬，寵愛貴盛，閎屢諫，忤旨。

translations.literal: At that time the favoured minister Dong Xian was Grand Marshal, doted on and noble and flourishing; Hong repeatedly remonstrated, opposing the intent.
translations.idiomatic: The favourite Dong Xian was grand marshal, flush with power and the emperor’s love; Wang Hong remonstrated again and again and earned imperial displeasure.

sentences.zh: 哀帝臨崩，以璽綬付賢曰：『無妄以與人。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ai was on the verge of death, he handed the seal-cord to Xian, saying: ‘Do not rashly give it to others.
translations.idiomatic: On his deathbed Emperor Ai pressed the imperial seal into Dong Xian’s hands with the words, ‘Do not hand this to anyone lightly.’

sentences.zh: 』時國無嗣主，內外恇懼，閎白元後，請奪之；

translations.literal: ’ At that time the state had no heir lord; inside and outside were fearful; Hong reported to the Grand Empress Dowager Yuan, requesting to seize it.
translations.idiomatic: ’ The realm had no heir; court and capital trembled. Wang Hong went to the grand empress dowager Yuan and asked leave to take the seals.

sentences.zh: 即帶劍至宣德後闥，舉手叱賢曰：『宮車晏駕，國嗣未立，公受恩深重，當俯伏號泣，何事久持璽綬以待禍至邪！

translations.literal: He immediately girded sword and went to the rear gate of Xuande, raised hand and rebuked Xian, saying: ‘The palace carriage has delayed its departure, the state heir is not yet established; you have received favour deep and heavy, ought to prostrate and wail; why matter of long holding seal-cord to await disaster's arrival!
translations.idiomatic: He buckled on a sword, strode to the inner gate of the Xuande hall, and shouted at Dong Xian: ‘The emperor is dead and no successor is named; you owe him everything—kneel and weep instead of clutching those cords until ruin finds you!’

sentences.zh: 』賢知閎必死，不敢拒之，乃跪授璽綬。

translations.literal: ’ Xian knew if he refused Hong he would die, did not dare resist him, then knelt and handed over the seal-cord.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Dong Xian knew Wang Hong would kill him if he refused; he knelt and surrendered the seals.

sentences.zh: 閎持上太后，朝廷壯之。

translations.literal: Hong carried them up to the empress dowager; the court thought him stalwart.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Hong carried them to the empress dowager, and the whole court applauded his nerve.

sentences.zh: 及王莽篡位，僭忌閎，乃出為東郡太守。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang usurped the throne, he was jealous of Hong in presumption, then sent him out as grand administrator of Dong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang seized the throne he envied Wang Hong’s stature and packed him off to be prefect of Dong commandery.

sentences.zh: 閎懼誅，常系藥手內。

translations.literal: Hong feared execution, constantly tied poison in his hand within.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing execution, he kept poison bound to his wrist.

sentences.zh: 莽敗，漢兵起，閎獨完全東郡三十餘萬戶，歸降更始。

translations.literal: When Mang was defeated, Han troops rose; Hong alone completely preserved Dong commandery's more than three hundred thousand households, returned and surrendered to Gengshi.
translations.idiomatic: When Mang fell and Han armies rose, Wang Hong alone kept Dong commandery intact—more than three hundred thousand households—and brought it over to the Gengshi court.

sentences.zh: 李憲者，潁川許昌人也。

translations.literal: Li Xian was a man of Xuchang in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian came from Xuchang in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 王莽時為廬江屬令。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's time he was subordinate magistrate of Lujiang.
translations.idiomatic: Under Wang Mang he served as an assistant magistrate in Lujiang.

sentences.zh: 莽末，江賊王州公等起眾十餘萬，攻掠郡縣，莽以憲為偏將軍、廬江連率，擊破州公。

translations.literal: At Mang's end, river bandits Wang Zhougong and others raised a multitude of more than one hundred thousand, attacked and plundered commanderies and counties; Mang appointed Xian as lieutenant-general and Lujiang chain leader, struck and defeated Zhougong.
translations.idiomatic: Near the end of Mang’s reign river pirates led by Wang Zhougong mustered more than a hundred thousand men and ravaged the countryside; Mang made Li Xian a lieutenant-general and chief of Lujiang, and he broke Wang Zhougong.

sentences.zh: 莽敗，憲據郡自守。

translations.literal: When Mang was defeated, Xian occupied the commandery and guarded himself.
translations.idiomatic: When Mang fell, Li Xian held the commandery on his own account.

sentences.zh: 更始元年，自稱淮南王。

translations.literal: In the first year of Gengshi, he styled himself King of Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Gengshi he proclaimed himself king of Huainan.

sentences.zh: 建武三年，遂自立為天子，置公卿百官，擁九城，眾十餘萬。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianwu, he thereupon established himself as Son of Heaven, set up dukes, ministers, and hundred officials, held nine cities, a multitude of more than one hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 3 he declared himself emperor, appointed a full bureaucracy, held nine cities, and commanded more than a hundred thousand men.

sentences.zh: 四年秋，光武幸壽春，遣揚武將軍馬成等擊憲，圍舒。

translations.literal: In autumn of the fourth year, Guangwu visited Shouchun, dispatched General Who Displays Might Ma Cheng and others to strike Xian, and besieged Shu.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn in Jianwu 4 Guangwu went to Shouchun and sent Ma Cheng, general who “displays might,” and others against Li Xian, investing the city of Shu.

sentences.zh: 至六年正月，拔之。

translations.literal: Reaching the first month of the sixth year, they took it.
translations.idiomatic: The city fell in the first month of Jianwu 6.

sentences.zh: 憲亡走，其軍士帛意，追斬憲而降，憲妻子皆伏誅。

translations.literal: Xian fled; his soldier Bo Yi pursued, beheaded Xian and surrendered; Xian's wife and children were all executed.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian fled; a soldier named Bo Yi ran him down, killed him, and surrendered. Li Xian’s wife and children were put to death.

sentences.zh: 封帛意漁侯。

translations.literal: Bo Yi was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Bo Yi was ennobled as marquis of Yu.

sentences.zh: 後憲餘黨淳于臨等猶聚眾數千人，屯灊山，攻殺安風令。

translations.literal: Later Xian's remnant faction Chunyu Lin and others still gathered a multitude of several thousand, encamped at Qian Mountain, attacked and killed the magistrate of Anfeng.
translations.idiomatic: Later, Li Xian’s followers such as Chunyu Lin rallied several thousand men on Mount Qian and killed the magistrate of Anfeng.

sentences.zh: 楊州牧歐陽歙遣兵不能克，帝議欲討之。

translations.literal: Shepherd of Yang Province Ouyang She dispatched troops but could not overcome; the emperor deliberated wishing to campaign against them.
translations.idiomatic: Ouyang She, shepherd of Yang province, sent troops without success, and the court debated a punitive expedition.

sentences.zh: 廬江人陳眾為從事，白歙請得喻降臨；

translations.literal: Chen Zhong of Lujiang was an aide; he reported to She requesting to be allowed to persuade Lin to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Zhong of Lujiang, an aide in Ouyang She’s staff, asked leave to talk Chunyu Lin into submission.

sentences.zh: 於是乘單車，駕白馬，往說而降之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he rode a single cart, drove a white horse, went and persuaded so that they surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: He drove out alone in a plain cart behind a white horse and talked them down.

sentences.zh: 灊山人共生為立祠，號『白馬陳從事』云。

translations.literal: The people of Qian Mountain together for him established a shrine, styled ‘White Horse Chen Aide,’ it is said.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Mount Qian raised a shrine to him and called him “Chen the aide on the white horse.”

sentences.zh: 彭寵字伯通，南陽宛人也。

translations.literal: Peng Chong, courtesy name Botong, was a man of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong, style Botong, was a native of Wan in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 父宏，哀帝時為漁陽太守，偉容貌，能飲飯，有威於邊。

translations.literal: His father Hong, in Emperor Ai's time was grand administrator of Yuyang, had grand appearance, could eat and drink much, had awe on the border.
translations.idiomatic: His father Peng Hong had been prefect of Yuyang under Emperor Ai—a imposing figure who ate and drank heavily and commanded respect on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 王莽居攝，誅不附己者，宏與何武、鮑宣並遇害。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang held the regency, he executed those who did not attach to himself; Hong together with He Wu and Bao Xuan together met harm.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang acted as regent he killed all who would not bend; Peng Hong died with He Wu and Bao Xuan.

sentences.zh: 寵少為郡吏，地皇中，為大司空士，從王邑東拒漢軍。

translations.literal: Chong in youth was a commandery clerk; in Dihuang, he was a clerk of the Grand Minister of Works, followed Wang Yi east to resist Han troops.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong began as a minor clerk in his commandery; in the Dihuang era he served on the staff of the grand minister of works and followed Wang Yi east to block the Han armies.

sentences.zh: 到洛陽，聞同產弟在漢兵中，懼誅，即與鄉人吳漢亡至漁陽，抵父時吏。

translations.literal: When he reached Luoyang, he heard his full brother was in Han troops, feared execution, immediately with fellow townsman Wu Han fled to Yuyang, and sought out his father's former clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Reaching Luoyang, he learned his own brother was with the Han forces and, fearing punishment, fled to Yuyang with his townsman Wu Han and threw himself on an old retainer of his father’s.

sentences.zh: 更始立，使謁者韓鴻持節徇北州，承制得專拜二千石已下。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he sent Herald Han Hong bearing credentials to bring the northern provinces to submission, by commission being able solely to appoint two-thousand-bushel and below.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Xuan took the throne he sent Han Hong north with credentials and authority to appoint officials up to the rank of two thousand piculs.

sentences.zh: 鴻至薊，以寵、漢並鄉閭故人，相見歡甚，即拜寵偏將軍，行漁陽太守事，漢安樂令。

translations.literal: When Hong reached Ji, because Chong and Han were both fellow-village old acquaintances, they met and rejoiced greatly; he immediately appointed Chong lieutenant-general, acting grand administrator of Yuyang affairs, Han as magistrate of Anle.
translations.idiomatic: At Ji, Han Hong found Peng Chong and Wu Han old neighbours from home; delighted at the reunion he named Peng Chong lieutenant-general and acting prefect of Yuyang and Wu Han magistrate of Anle.

sentences.zh: 及光武鎮慰河北，至薊，以書招寵。

translations.literal: When Guangwu pacified and comforted Hebei, reached Ji, he used a letter to summon Chong.
translations.idiomatic: When Guangwu was pacifying the north and came to Ji, he wrote to summon Peng Chong.

sentences.zh: 寵具牛、酒，將上謁。

translations.literal: Chong prepared oxen and wine, was about to go up to audience.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong laid in oxen and wine and prepared to call on him.

sentences.zh: 會王郎詐立，傳檄燕、趙，遣將徇漁陽、上谷，急發其兵，北州眾多疑惑，欲從之。

translations.literal: It happened that Wang Lang falsely established himself, transmitted proclamations to Yan and Zhao, dispatched generals to bring Yuyang and Shanggu to submission, urgently raised their troops; the northern provinces' multitudes much doubted, wished to follow him.
translations.idiomatic: Then Wang Lang seized Handan, sent circulars through Yan and Zhao, and ordered generals to press Yuyang and Shanggu for troops; the whole north wavered and many meant to join him.

sentences.zh: 吳漢說寵從光武，語在《漢傳》。

translations.literal: Wu Han persuaded Chong to follow Guangwu; the words are in the 'Han' biography.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han’s persuasion of Peng Chong to follow Guangwu is told in Wu Han’s biography.

sentences.zh: 會上谷太守耿況亦使功曹寇恂詣寵，結謀共歸光武。

translations.literal: It happened that grand administrator of Shanggu Geng Kuang also sent merit clerk Kou Xun to visit Chong, knotted plans jointly to return to Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: About the same time Geng Kuang of Shanggu sent his aide Kou Xun to Peng Chong, and the two plotted together to join Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 寵乃發步騎三千人，以吳漢行長史，及都尉嚴宣、護軍蓋延、狐奴令王梁，與上谷軍合而南，及光武於廣阿。

translations.literal: Chong then sent three thousand foot and cavalry, with Wu Han acting chief clerk, and commandant Yan Xuan, protector of the army Gai Yan, magistrate of Hunu Wang Liang, combined with Shanggu army and went south, joined Guangwu at Guang'a.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong therefore sent three thousand foot and horse under Wu Han as acting chief clerk, with Commandant Yan Xuan, protector Gai Yan, and Wang Liang, magistrate of Hunu, marched south with the Shanggu column, and met Guangwu at Guang’a.

sentences.zh: 光武承制封寵建忠侯，賜號大將軍。

translations.literal: Guangwu by commission enfeoffed Chong as Marquis Who Establishes Loyalty, bestowed the title Grand General.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu, acting under emergency powers, ennobled him as marquis who “establishes loyalty” and gave him the title grand general.

sentences.zh: 遂圍邯鄲，寵轉糧食，前後不絕。

translations.literal: Thereupon they besieged Handan; Chong transported grain and food, front and rear without break.
translations.idiomatic: During the siege of Handan he kept grain convoys moving without a break.

sentences.zh: 及王郎死，光武追銅馬，北至薊。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang died, Guangwu pursued the Bronze Horses, north reached Ji.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Lang fell, Guangwu pursued the Bronze Horse band north as far as Ji.

sentences.zh: 寵上謁，自負其功，意望甚高，光武接之不能滿，以此懷不平。

translations.literal: Chong went up to audience, bore himself on his merit, his hopes were very high; Guangwu received him and could not satisfy him; by this he harboured discontent.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong came to court swollen with pride in what he had done; Guangwu’s welcome fell short of his expectations, and resentment lodged in his breast.

sentences.zh: 光武知之，以問幽州牧朱浮。

translations.literal: Guangwu knew it, used it to ask Shepherd of You Province Zhu Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu noticed and asked Zhu Fu, shepherd of You province, about it.

sentences.zh: 浮對曰：『前吳漢北發兵時，大王遺寵以所服劍，又倚以為北道主人。

translations.literal: Fu replied, saying: ‘Formerly when Wu Han northward raised troops, the great king presented Chong with the sword he wore, and moreover relied on him as master of the northern road.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu answered: ‘When Wu Han marched north you gave Peng Chong the sword from your own belt and treated him as your host on the northern road.’

sentences.zh: 寵謂至當迎閤握手，交歡並坐。

translations.literal: Chong thought on arrival he ought to be met at the gate, hands clasped, exchange joy and sit together.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong expected to be met at the gate, hands clasped with yours, and seated as an equal.’

sentences.zh: 今既不然，所以失望。

translations.literal: Now since it is not so, that is why there is disappointment.
translations.idiomatic: When that did not happen, he was bound to feel slighted.’

sentences.zh: 』浮因曰：『王莽為宰衡時，甄豐旦夕入謀議，時人語曰：「夜半客，甄長伯。

translations.literal: ’ Fu then said: ‘When Wang Mang was chief regent, Zhen Feng morning and evening entered to deliberate plans; people of the time had a saying: “Midnight guest, Zhen the elder brother.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Zhu Fu added: ‘When Wang Mang was chief regent, Zhen Feng was in his councils day and night, and men said, “If a guest comes at midnight, it is Elder Zhen.”’

sentences.zh: 」及莽篡位後，豐意不平，卒以誅死。

translations.literal: ” And when after Mang usurped the throne, Feng's mind was not level, in the end by execution died.
translations.idiomatic: ’ After the usurpation Zhen Feng nursed a grievance and was executed in the end.’

sentences.zh: 』光武大笑，以為不至於此。

translations.literal: ’ Guangwu greatly laughed, thought it would not reach this.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Guangwu laughed and said it would never come to that.

sentences.zh: 及即位，吳漢、王梁，寵之所遣，並為三公，而寵獨無所加，愈怏怏不得志。

translations.literal: When he ascended the throne, Wu Han, Wang Liang, whom Chong had dispatched, together became the Three Dukes, but Chong alone had nothing added; increasingly disgruntled, he could not achieve his will.
translations.idiomatic: Yet when Guangwu took the throne, Wu Han and Wang Liang—both men Peng Chong had sent—rose to the three highest offices while Peng Chong received no further honour; he grew ever more sullen.

sentences.zh: 嘆曰：『我功當為王；

translations.literal: He sighed, saying: ‘My merit ought to be king.
translations.idiomatic: He sighed, ‘By my deeds I ought to be a king.’

sentences.zh: 但爾者，陛下忘我邪？』

translations.literal: Only thus—has Your Majesty forgotten me?’
translations.idiomatic: Is this all I am to have—has His Majesty forgotten me?’

sentences.zh: 是時，北州破散，而漁陽差完，有舊鹽鐵官，寵轉以貿谷，積珍寶，益富強，朱浮與寵不相能，浮數譖構之。

translations.literal: At this time the northern provinces were broken and scattered, but Yuyang was comparatively intact, had old salt and iron offices; Chong exchanged them for grain, piled up treasures, increasingly grew rich and strong; Zhu Fu with Chong did not get along; Fu repeatedly slandered and framed him.
translations.idiomatic: The north was in ruins while Yuyang remained almost whole; its old salt and iron monopoly let Peng Chong trade for grain, heap up treasure, and grow rich and strong. Zhu Fu could not abide him and slandered him repeatedly.

sentences.zh: 建武二年春，詔征寵，寵意浮賣己，上疏願與浮俱征。

translations.literal: In spring of the second year of Jianwu, an edict summoned Chong; Chong thought Fu had sold him, submitted a memorial wishing to go to court together with Fu.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 2 an edict summoned him to court; Peng Chong decided Zhu Fu had denounced him and memorialized that he would obey only if Zhu Fu went with him.

sentences.zh: 又與吳漢、蓋延等書，盛言浮枉狀，固求同征。

translations.literal: Again he wrote to Wu Han, Gai Yan and others, greatly stating Fu's wrongful condition, firmly requesting to go to court together.
translations.idiomatic: He wrote at length to Wu Han, Gai Yan, and others accusing Zhu Fu of malice and again demanded that Fu accompany him.

sentences.zh: 帝不許，益以自疑。

translations.literal: The emperor did not permit; increasingly by this he doubted himself.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor refused, and Peng Chong’s suspicions deepened.

sentences.zh: 而其妻素剛，不堪抑屈，固勸無受召。

translations.literal: Moreover his wife by nature was rigid, could not bear suppression and bending, firmly urged not to accept the summons.
translations.idiomatic: His wife was a woman of fierce temper who could not bear to be humbled and urged him to ignore the summons.

sentences.zh: 寵又與常所親信吏計議，皆懷怨於浮，莫有勸行者。

translations.literal: Chong again with clerks he constantly trusted and was intimate with deliberated; all harboured resentment toward Fu; none had one who urged going.
translations.idiomatic: He took counsel with his favourite officers; every one of them nursed a grudge against Zhu Fu, and none urged him to obey.

sentences.zh: 帝遣寵從弟子後蘭卿喻之，寵因留子後蘭卿，遂發兵反，拜署將帥，自將二萬餘人攻朱浮於薊，分兵徇廣陽、上谷、右北平。

translations.literal: The emperor sent Chong's cousin's son Hou Lanqing to instruct him; Chong thereupon detained his son Hou Lanqing, then raised troops and rebelled, appointed generals, personally led more than twenty thousand men to attack Zhu Fu at Ji, divided troops to bring Guangyang, Shanggu, and Youbeiping to submission.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent Hou Lanqing, a cousin’s son of Peng Chong, to reason with him; Peng Chong held the youth hostage, rose in revolt, commissioned his commanders, led over twenty thousand men against Zhu Fu at Ji, and sent columns to overrun Guangyang, Shanggu, and Youbeiping.

sentences.zh: 又自與耿況俱有重功，而恩賞並薄，數遣使要誘況。

translations.literal: Moreover he himself together with Geng Kuang both had heavy merit, yet favour and rewards were alike thin; he repeatedly dispatched envoys to invite and entice Kuang.
translations.idiomatic: He reminded himself that he and Geng Kuang had both done great service yet received scant reward, and he sent envoys again and again to lure Geng Kuang to his side.

sentences.zh: 況不受，輒斬其使。

translations.literal: Kuang did not accept, immediately beheaded his envoys.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Kuang would not listen and executed every messenger.

sentences.zh: 秋，帝使遊擊將軍鄧隆救薊。

translations.literal: In autumn, the emperor sent General of Roaming Attack Deng Long to rescue Ji.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Guangwu sent Deng Long, general of roaming attack, to relieve Ji.

sentences.zh: 隆軍潞南，浮軍雍奴，遣吏奏狀。

translations.literal: Long's army was south of Lu; Fu's army was at Yongnu; he dispatched clerks to memorialize the situation.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Long camped south of the Lu River, Zhu Fu at Yongnu, and they sent clerks to report their dispositions.

sentences.zh: 帝讀檄，怒謂使吏曰：『營相去百里，其勢豈可得相及？

translations.literal: The emperor read the dispatch, angrily said to the envoy clerk: ‘The camps are apart a hundred li; in that situation how can they possibly reach each other?
translations.idiomatic: Reading the report, the emperor snapped at the messenger: ‘Your camps stand a hundred li apart—how do you expect to support each other?’

sentences.zh: 比若還，北軍必敗矣。

translations.literal: By the time you return, the northern army will surely be defeated.
translations.idiomatic: By the time word comes back, the northern army will already be lost.’

sentences.zh: 』寵果盛兵臨河以拒隆，又別發輕騎三千襲其後，大破隆軍。

translations.literal: ’ Chong indeed massed troops along the river to resist Long, also separately sent three thousand light cavalry to raid his rear, greatly defeated Long's army.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Peng Chong massed troops along the river to block Deng Long and sent three thousand light horse around his rear, shattering Long’s army.

sentences.zh: 浮遠，遂不能救，引而去。

translations.literal: Fu was far, therefore could not rescue, led away and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu was too distant to help and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 明年春，寵遂拔右北平、上谷數縣。

translations.literal: The next spring, Chong thereupon took several counties of Youbeiping and Shanggu.
translations.idiomatic: The following spring he seized several counties in Youbeiping and Shanggu.

sentences.zh: 遣使以美女繒彩賂遺匈奴，要結和親。

translations.literal: He dispatched envoys with beautiful women and silks to bribe and present to the Xiongnu, inviting to knot marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: He sent envoys with gifts of women and silk to buy the Xiongnu chieftains and seal a marriage alliance.

sentences.zh: 單于使左南將軍七八千騎，往來為遊兵以助寵。

translations.literal: The Shanyu ordered the General of the Left South seven or eight thousand cavalry, coming and going as roaming troops to assist Chong.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu detached seven or eight thousand horsemen under the general of the Left South to ride as flying columns in support of Peng Chong.

sentences.zh: 又南結張步及富平獲索諸豪傑，皆與交質連衡。

translations.literal: He also southward allied with Zhang Bu and the Fuping and Huosuo various heroes, all exchanged hostages and joined in horizontal alliance.
translations.idiomatic: He also courted Zhang Bu and the bandit chiefs of Fuping and Huosuo in the south, exchanging hostages and forging leagues.

sentences.zh: 遂攻拔薊城，自立為燕王。

translations.literal: Thereupon he attacked and took Ji city, established himself as King of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: He stormed Ji and proclaimed himself king of Yan.

sentences.zh: 其妻數惡夢，又多見怪變，卜筮及望氣者皆言兵當從中起。

translations.literal: His wife repeatedly had evil dreams, and also often saw strange transformations; diviners by tortoise-shell and stalk and those who read vapours all said arms ought to rise from within.
translations.idiomatic: His wife was plagued by nightmares and omens; tortoise and milfoil diviners and men who read the winds all warned that mutiny would come from within the household.

sentences.zh: 寵疑子後蘭卿質漢歸，故不信之，使將兵居外，無親於中。

translations.literal: Chong suspected his son Hou Lanqing had been hostage in Han and returned, therefore did not trust him, ordered him to lead troops and dwell outside, no intimacy within.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong distrusted his son Hou Lanqing, who had been a hostage at court and come home, kept him outside the city at the head of troops, and would not let him near the inner quarters.

sentences.zh: 五年春，寵齋，獨在便室。

translations.literal: In spring of the fifth year, Chong fasted, alone was in the privy chamber.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Jianwu 5 he was observing a fast and sat alone in a side chamber.

sentences.zh: 蒼頭子密等三人因寵臥寐，共縛著床，告外吏云：『大王齋禁，皆使吏休。

translations.literal: The black-headed Zimi and three others, because Chong lay sleeping, together bound him to the bed, told outer clerks, saying: ‘The great king fasts under prohibition; all order clerks to rest.
translations.idiomatic: Three housemen led by Zimi found him asleep, tied him to the couch, and told the clerks outside, ‘The king is fasting; everyone may leave.’

sentences.zh: 』偽稱寵命教，收縛奴婢，各置一處。

translations.literal: ’ Falsely claiming Chong's command instruction, they seized and bound male and female slaves, each placed in one place.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Forging his orders they rounded up the household slaves and locked them away by groups.

sentences.zh: 又以寵命呼其妻。

translations.literal: Again using Chong's command they summoned his wife.
translations.idiomatic: They then called his wife in Peng Chong’s name.

sentences.zh: 妻入，大驚。

translations.literal: The wife entered, greatly startled.
translations.idiomatic: She entered and cried out in terror.

sentences.zh: 寵急呼曰：『趣為諸將軍辦裝。

translations.literal: Chong urgently called, saying: ‘Hasten to prepare baggage for the generals.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong shouted, ‘Quick—pack the generals’ baggage!’

sentences.zh: 』於是兩奴將妻入取寶物，留一奴守寵。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon two slaves led the wife in to fetch treasures, left one slave to guard Chong.
translations.idiomatic: ’ Two slaves marched her off to gather valuables while a third stayed with the bound king.

sentences.zh: 寵謂守奴曰：『若小兒，我素愛也，今為子密所迫劫耳。

translations.literal: Chong said to the guarding slave: ‘You, young lad, I have always loved; now it is only that you are forced and coerced by Zimi.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong said to the guard, ‘Boy, I have always favoured you; Zimi has forced you into this.’

sentences.zh: 解我縛，當以女珠妻汝，家中財物皆與若。

translations.literal: Release my bonds; I shall take daughter Zhu as wife for you; all wealth goods in the house I give to you.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Loosen my cords and I will give you my daughter Zhu in marriage and everything I own.’

sentences.zh: 』小奴意欲解之，視戶外，見子密聽其語，遂不敢解。

translations.literal: ’ The young slave's mind wished to release them; looking outside the door, saw Zimi listening to his words, then did not dare release.
translations.idiomatic: ’ The boy almost yielded, but glanced through the door, saw Zimi listening, and dared not.

sentences.zh: 於是收金玉衣物，至寵所裝之，被馬六匹，使妻縫兩縑囊。

translations.literal: Thereupon they gathered gold, jade, and clothing, came to Chong's place and packed them, caparisoned six horses, ordered the wife to sew two silk bags.
translations.idiomatic: They piled gold, jade, and garments at Peng Chong’s feet, saddled six horses, and made his wife stitch two silk sacks.

sentences.zh: 昏夜後，解寵手，令作記告城門將軍云：『今遣子密等至子後蘭卿所，速開門出，勿稽留之。

translations.literal: After dark night, they released Chong's hands, ordered him to compose a note telling the gate general, saying: ‘Now I dispatch Zimi and others to son Hou Lanqing's place; quickly open the gate and let them out; do not delay and detain them.
translations.idiomatic: After dark they freed his hands and made him write an order to the gate captain: ‘I am sending Zimi and his party to Hou Lanqing; open the gate at once and let them pass without delay.’

sentences.zh: 』書成，即斬寵及妻頭，置囊中，便持記馳出城，因以詣闕。

translations.literal: ’ When the writing was finished, they immediately beheaded Chong and his wife's heads, placed them in the bags, then holding the note galloped out of the city, thereby went to the palace gate.
translations.idiomatic: ’ When the note was written they cut off Peng Chong’s head and his wife’s, stuffed both into the sacks, spurred through the gates with the pass, and rode for the capital.

sentences.zh: 封為不義侯。

translations.literal: He was enfeoffed as Marquis of Unrighteousness.
translations.idiomatic: The court ennobled him as marquis of “unrighteousness.”

sentences.zh: 明旦，閤門不開，官屬逾墻而入，見寵屍，驚怖。

translations.literal: At dawn the compound gate did not open; officials climbed the wall and entered, saw Chong's corpse, were frightened.
translations.idiomatic: At daybreak the inner gates stayed shut; his officers scaled the wall, found Peng Chong’s body, and were stricken with horror.

sentences.zh: 其尚書韓立等共立寵子午為王，以子後蘭卿為將軍。

translations.literal: His Secretary Han Li and others together established Chong's son Wu as king, appointed son Hou Lanqing as general.
translations.idiomatic: His secretary Han Li and the rest set up Peng Chong’s son Peng Wu as king and named Hou Lanqing a general.

sentences.zh: 國師韓利斬午首，詣征虜將軍祭遵降。

translations.literal: State Teacher Han Li beheaded Wu's head, went to General Who Conquers Captives Ji Zun and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Han Li, the state instructor, struck off Peng Wu’s head and surrendered to Ji Zun, general who conquers captives.

sentences.zh: 夷其宗族。

translations.literal: Exterminated his clan and kin.
translations.idiomatic: His whole clan was extirpated.

sentences.zh: 盧芳字君期，安定三水人入，居左谷中。

translations.literal: Lu Fang, courtesy name Junqi, was a man of Sanshui in Anding, entered, dwelt in Zuogu valley.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang, style Junqi, came from Sanshui in Anding and lived in the Zuogu valley.

sentences.zh: 王莽時，天下咸思漢德，芳由是詐自稱武帝曾孫劉文伯。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's time, all under heaven longed for Han virtue; Fang on this account falsely styled himself Emperor Wu's great-grandson Liu Wenbo.
translations.idiomatic: While Wang Mang held the realm, men everywhere missed the virtue of Han; Lu Fang therefore passed himself off as Liu Wenbo, a great-grandson of Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 曾祖母匈奴谷蠡渾邪王之姊為武帝皇后，生三子。

translations.literal: His great-grandmother was the Xiongnu Guili Hunye King's elder sister who became Emperor Wu's empress; she bore three sons.
translations.idiomatic: His alleged great-grandmother was the sister of the Xiongnu prince Hunye of the Guili rank, who became Emperor Wu’s empress and bore three sons.

sentences.zh: 遭江充之亂，太子誅，皇后坐死，中子次卿亡之長陵，小子回卿逃於左谷。

translations.literal: They suffered Jiang Chong's turmoil; the heir was executed; the empress died by implication; the middle son Ciqing fled to Changling; the youngest son Huiqing fled to Zuogu.
translations.idiomatic: When Jiang Chong’s intrigue struck down the heir, the empress died with him; the middle son, Ciqing, fled to Changling and the youngest, Huiqing, to the Zuogu valley.

sentences.zh: 霍將軍立次卿，迎回卿。

translations.literal: General Huo established Ciqing, welcomed Huiqing.
translations.idiomatic: General Huo Guang set up Ciqing and sent for Huiqing.

sentences.zh: 回卿不出，因居左谷，生子孫卿，孫卿生文伯。

translations.literal: Huiqing did not come out, therefore dwelt in Zuogu, bore a son Sunqing; Sunqing bore Wenbo.
translations.idiomatic: Huiqing would not emerge and stayed on in Zuogu; his line ran Sunqing and then Wenbo.

sentences.zh: 常以是言誑惑安定間。

translations.literal: He constantly used these words to deceive and confuse within Anding.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang peddled this tale through Anding for years.

sentences.zh: 王莽末，乃與三水屬國羌胡起兵。

translations.literal: At Wang Mang's end, then together with the dependent state's Qiang and Hu of Sanshui he raised troops.
translations.idiomatic: At the close of Wang Mang’s reign he rose with the Qiang and Hu of the Sanshui dependent state.

sentences.zh: 更始至長安，征芳為騎都尉，使鎮撫安定以西。

translations.literal: When Gengshi reached Chang'an, he summoned Fang as cavalry commandant, ordered him to pacify and comfort west of Anding.
translations.idiomatic: When the Gengshi court entered Chang’an it summoned Lu Fang as cavalry commandant and sent him to pacify the country west of Anding.

sentences.zh: 更始敗，三水豪傑共計議，以芳劉氏子孫，宜承宗廟，乃共立芳為上將軍、西平王，使使與西羌、匈奴結和親。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was defeated, the heroes of Sanshui jointly deliberated, considering Fang a Liu clan descendant ought to receive the ancestral temples; they together established Fang as supreme general and King of Xiping, sent envoys with western Qiang and Xiongnu to knot marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: After Liu Xuan fell, the magnates of Sanshui agreed that Lu Fang, as a Liu, ought to carry the imperial temples; they named him supreme general and king of Xiping and sent envoys to treat with the western Qiang and the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 單于曰：『匈怒本與漢約為兄弟。
