translations.literal: The Shanyu said: ‘Xiongnu originally with Han made covenant as brothers.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu said, ‘The Xiongnu and Han were once sworn as brothers.’

sentences.zh: 後匈奴中衰，呼韓邪單于歸漢，漢為發兵擁護，世世稱臣。

translations.literal: Later the Xiongnu middle declined; Shanyu Huhanye returned to Han; Han for him raised troops to protect; generation after generation called themselves ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when the Xiongnu waned, Chanyu Huhanye submitted to Han; Han sent armies to shield him, and for generations his people called themselves vassals.

sentences.zh: 今漢亦中絕，劉氏來歸我，亦當立之，令尊事我。

translations.literal: Now Han also is cut off in the middle; the Liu clan comes to submit to me; I also ought to establish him, order him to honour and serve me.
translations.idiomatic: Now Han’s line is broken; the Lius come to me for shelter—I should set one of them up and make him honour my authority.’

sentences.zh: 』乃使句林王將數千騎迎芳，芳與兄禽、弟程俱入匈奴。

translations.literal: ’ Thereupon he ordered the Goulin King to lead several thousand cavalry to welcome Fang; Fang together with elder brother Qin, younger brother Cheng, all entered the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: ’ He sent the Goulin king with thousands of riders to escort Lu Fang; Lu Fang entered the steppe with his brothers Qin and Cheng.

sentences.zh: 單于遂立芳為漢帝。

translations.literal: The Shanyu thereupon established Fang as Han emperor.
translations.idiomatic: The chanyu thereupon proclaimed him emperor of Han.

sentences.zh: 以程為中郎將，將胡騎還入安定。

translations.literal: He appointed Cheng as gentleman of the palace, led Hu cavalry back into Anding.
translations.idiomatic: He made Cheng a gentleman of the palace and sent him back into Anding at the head of Hu cavalry.

sentences.zh: 初，五原人李興、隨昱，朔方人田颯，代郡人石鮪、閔堪，各起兵自稱將軍。

translations.literal: Earlier, Li Xing and Sui Yu of Wuyuan, Tian Sa of Shuofang, Shi Wei and Min Kan of Dai commandery, each raised troops and styled themselves generals.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xing and Sui Yu of Wuyuan, Tian Sa of Shuofang, Shi Wei and Min Kan of Dai had each raised bands and called themselves generals.

sentences.zh: 建武四年，單于遣無樓且渠王入五原塞，與李興等和親，告興欲令芳還漢地為帝。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Jianwu, the Shanyu dispatched the Wulou Qielü King to enter the Wuyuan pass, with Li Xing and others to knot marriage alliance, told Xing he wished to order Fang to return to Han territory to be emperor.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 4 the chanyu sent the Wulou Qielü king through the Wuyuan frontier to treat with Li Xing and his allies, asking them to bring Lu Fang back into Han lands as emperor.

sentences.zh: 五年，李興、閔堪引兵至單于庭迎芳，與俱入塞，都九原縣。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, Li Xing and Min Kan led troops to the Shanyu court to welcome Fang, together with him entered the pass, made capital at Jiuyuan county.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 5 Li Xing and Min Kan marched to the chanyu’s court, escorted Lu Fang through the passes, and set up their capital at Jiuyuan.

sentences.zh: 掠有五原、朔方、雲中、定襄、雁門五郡，並置守令，與胡通兵，侵苦北邊。

translations.literal: They plundered and held Wuyuan, Shuofang, Yunzhong, Dingxiang, and Yanmen five commanderies, together set up guardians and magistrates, communicated troops with the Hu, harassed the northern border.
translations.idiomatic: They overran the five northern commanderies of Wuyuan, Shuofang, Yunzhong, Dingxiang, and Yanmen, appointed their own prefects, coordinated with the Hu, and tormented the frontier.

sentences.zh: 六年，芳將軍賈覽將胡騎擊殺代郡太守劉興。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, Fang's general Jia Lan led Hu cavalry to strike and kill grand administrator of Dai commandery Liu Xing.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 6 his general Jia Lan led Hu horsemen against Dai and killed Prefect Liu Xing.

sentences.zh: 芳後以事誅其五原太守李興兄弟，而其朔方太守田颯、雲中太守橋扈恐懼，叛芳，舉郡降，光武令領職如故。

translations.literal: Later Fang for an affair executed his Wuyuan grand administrator Li Xing and brothers; but his Shuofang grand administrator Tian Sa and Yunzhong grand administrator Qiao Hu were afraid, rebelled against Fang, raised the commandery to surrender; Guangwu ordered them to hold office as before.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang later executed Li Xing, his prefect of Wuyuan, and Li’s brothers; Tian Sa of Shuofang and Qiao Hu of Yunzhong, terrified, broke with him and surrendered their commanderies, and Guangwu let them keep their posts.

sentences.zh: 後大司馬吳漢、驃騎大將軍杜茂數擊芳，並不克。

translations.literal: Later Grand Marshal Wu Han and Grand General of Agile Cavalry Du Mao repeatedly struck Fang, together did not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: Grand marshal Wu Han and grand general of agile cavalry Du Mao attacked him more than once without success.

sentences.zh: 十二年，芳與賈覽共攻雲中，久不下，其將隨昱留守九原，欲脅芳降。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, Fang together with Jia Lan jointly attacked Yunzhong, long could not take it; his general Sui Yu remained guarding Jiuyuan, wished to coerce Fang to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 12 he and Jia Lan besieged Yunzhong for a long time without taking it; his general Sui Yu, left to hold Jiuyuan, meant to force him to submit to Han.

sentences.zh: 芳知羽翼外附，心膂內離，遂棄輜重，與十餘騎亡入匈奴，其眾盡歸隨昱。

translations.literal: Fang knew wings and feathers attached outside, heart and backbone parted within, thereupon abandoned baggage trains, with more than ten cavalry fled into the Xiongnu; his multitude all returned to Sui Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing his allies peel away and his inner circle split, he abandoned his trains and fled to the Xiongnu with a dozen riders while his army went over to Sui Yu.

sentences.zh: 昱乃隨使者程恂詣闕。

translations.literal: Yu then followed envoy Cheng Xun to the palace gate.
translations.idiomatic: Sui Yu then followed the envoy Cheng Xun to the capital.

sentences.zh: 拜昱為五原太守，封鐫胡侯，昱弟憲武進侯。

translations.literal: Yu was appointed grand administrator of Wuyuan, enfeoffed as Marquis Who Chisels the Hu; Yu's younger brother Xian was Marquis of Wujin.
translations.idiomatic: He was named prefect of Wuyuan and marquis who “chisels the Hu”; his brother Sui Xian received the marquisate of Wujin.

sentences.zh: 十六年，芳復入居高柳，與閔堪兄林使使請降。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Fang again entered and dwelt at Gaoliu, together with Min Kan's elder brother Lin sent envoys requesting surrender.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 16 he reappeared at Gaoliu and, with Min Kan’s brother Min Lin, sent envoys to offer submission.

sentences.zh: 乃立芳為代王，堪為代相，林為代太傅，賜繒二萬匹，因使和集匈奴。

translations.literal: Thereupon they established Fang as King of Dai, Kan as chancellor of Dai, Lin as grand tutor of Dai, bestowed twenty thousand bolts of silk, thereby ordered him to harmonize and gather the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: The court made him king of Dai, Min Kan his chancellor, Min Lin his grand tutor, gave him twenty thousand bolts of silk, and charged him to conciliate the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 芳上疏謝曰：『臣芳過托先帝遺體，棄在邊陲。

translations.literal: Fang submitted a memorial thanking, saying: ‘Your subject Fang wrongly relied on the former emperor's bodily legacy, was cast aside on the border.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang memorialized in gratitude: ‘I falsely claimed descent from the late emperor and was cast out upon the marches.’

sentences.zh: 社稷遭王莽廢絕，以是子孫之憂，所宜共誅，故遂西連羌戎，北懷匈奴。

translations.literal: The altars of soil and grain encountered Wang Mang's abolition and cutting off; by this the descendants' sorrow, what ought jointly to be executed; therefore I then west linked Qiang and Rong, north embraced the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: ‘When Wang Mang cut off the Han shrines, every true son of the house should have risen against him; I therefore turned west to the Qiang and Rong and north to the Xiongnu.’

sentences.zh: 單于不忘舊德，權立救助，是時兵革並起，往往而在。

translations.literal: The Shanyu did not forget old virtue, by expedient established aid; at that time arms and leathers arose together, often were present.
translations.idiomatic: ‘The chanyu, remembering old kindness, lent me a borrowed throne while war flared on every side.’

sentences.zh: 臣非敢有所貪覬，期於奉承宗廟，興立社稷，是以久僭號位，十有餘年，罪宜萬死。

translations.literal: Your subject does not dare have greedy longing; I hoped to receive and uphold the ancestral temples, raise and establish the altars of soil and grain; therefore long usurped title and position, more than ten years; guilt ought to be ten thousand deaths.
translations.idiomatic: ‘I never coveted power for myself—only meant to uphold the temples of Han; yet I bore a usurped title for more than ten years and deserve death ten thousand times over.’

sentences.zh: 陛下聖德高明，躬率眾賢，海內賓服，惠及殊俗。

translations.literal: Your Majesty's sagely virtue is high and bright, personally leads the multitude of worthies; within the seas guest-submission; favour reaches to strange customs.
translations.idiomatic: ‘Your Majesty’s sacred virtue draws all the worthy to you; within the seas men submit, and your grace reaches even to barbarians.’

sentences.zh: 以胏附之故，赦臣芳罪，加以仁恩，封為代王，使備北藩。

translations.literal: Because of the reason of close flesh attachment, pardoned your subject Fang's crime, added benevolent favour, enfeoffed as King of Dai, ordered to prepare the northern screen.
translations.idiomatic: ‘As one near in kin to the throne you have pardoned my crimes, heaped kindness on me, made me king of Dai, and set me to guard the northern marches.’

sentences.zh: 無以報塞重責，冀必欲和輯匈奴，不敢遺餘力，負恩貸。

translations.literal: I have no means to repay and block the heavy responsibility; I hope surely to wish to harmonize and gather the Xiongnu, dare not leave remaining strength, betray favour lent.
translations.idiomatic: ‘I cannot repay such a charge except by doing all in my power to reconcile the Xiongnu and never slackening in gratitude for your mercy.’

sentences.zh: 謹奉天子玉璽，思望闕庭。

translations.literal: I respectfully present the Son of Heaven's jade seal, long to gaze at the palace court.
translations.idiomatic: ‘I hereby return the imperial jade seal and long to look upon your court.’

sentences.zh: 』詔報芳朝明年正月。

translations.literal: ’ An edict replied to Fang to court in the first month of next year.
translations.idiomatic: ’ An edict bade him attend court the following first month.

sentences.zh: 其冬，芳入朝，南及昌平，有詔止，令更朝明歲。

translations.literal: That winter, Fang entered to court, south reached Changping; there was an edict to stop, ordered to court again next year.
translations.idiomatic: That winter, as he travelled south for his audience and reached Changping, an edict stopped him and put the visit off until the next year.

sentences.zh: 芳自道還，憂恐，乃復背叛，遂反，與閔堪、閔林相攻連月。

translations.literal: Fang on returning by the road, anxious and afraid, then again betrayed, thereupon rebelled, with Min Kan and Min Lin mutually attacked for successive months.
translations.idiomatic: Turned back on the road, he grew fearful, rebelled again, and for months fought Min Kan and Min Lin.

sentences.zh: 匈媽遣數百騎迎芳及妻子出塞。

translations.literal: Xiongnu mother dispatched several hundred cavalry to welcome Fang and wife and children out of the pass.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu sent several hundred riders to escort Lu Fang and his family beyond the frontier.

sentences.zh: 芳留匈奴中十餘年，病死。

translations.literal: Fang remained among the Xiongnu more than ten years, died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: He remained on the steppe for another decade and died of illness.

sentences.zh: 初，安定屬國胡與芳為寇，及芳敗，胡人還鄉里，積苦縣官徭役。

translations.literal: Earlier, the dependent state's Hu of Anding with Fang were bandits; when Fang was defeated, the Hu people returned to villages and hamlets, long suffered county official corvée labour.
translations.idiomatic: The Hu of the Anding dependent state had raided with Lu Fang; after his fall they went home, only to groan under corvée exactions from the magistrates.

sentences.zh: 其中有駁馬少伯者，素剛壯；

translations.literal: Among them was one Boma Shaobo, by nature rigid and strong.
translations.idiomatic: Among them was a man called Boma Shaobo, fierce by nature.

sentences.zh: 二十一年，遂率種人反叛，與匈奴連和，屯聚青山。

translations.literal: In the twenty-first year, he then led his kindred people to rebel, allied with the Xiongnu, encamped and gathered at Green Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-first year of Jianwu he led his tribesmen in revolt, allied with the Xiongnu, and massed on Mount Qing.

sentences.zh: 乃遺將兵長史陳訢，率三千騎擊之，少伯乃降。

translations.literal: Thereupon he left behind commanding-troops chief clerk Chen Xin, led three thousand cavalry to strike them; Shaobo then surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: The court sent Chen Xin, chief clerk in charge of troops, with three thousand horse against him, and Shaobo submitted.

sentences.zh: 徙於冀縣。

translations.literal: He was moved to Ji county.
translations.idiomatic: He was resettled in Ji county.

sentences.zh: 論曰：傳稱『盛德必百世祀』，孔子曰『寬則得眾』。

translations.literal: The disquisition says: The tradition states ‘Great virtue must have sacrifice for a hundred generations’; Confucius said ‘With leniency one obtains the multitude.’
translations.idiomatic: The historian’s judgment: The classics say, ‘Great virtue wins sacrifice for a hundred generations,’ and Confucius said, ‘The lenient ruler wins the people.’

sentences.zh: 夫能得眾心，則百世不忘矣。

translations.literal: If one can obtain the multitude's hearts, then a hundred generations will not forget.
translations.idiomatic: Win the people’s hearts, and they will remember you for generations.

sentences.zh: 觀更始之際，劉氏之遺恩餘烈，英雄豈能抗之哉！

translations.literal: Observe at the time of Gengshi, the Liu clan's remaining favour and surviving fierce blaze—how could heroes resist it!
translations.idiomatic: Look at the Gengshi years: the lingering grace of the house of Liu—what rival could stand against that tide?

sentences.zh: 然則知高祖、孝文之寬仁，結於人心深矣。

translations.literal: Then it is known that Gaozu and Xiaowen's lenient benevolence, knotted in people's hearts, was deep.
translations.idiomatic: So we see how deeply Gaozu and Emperor Wen bound the realm with mercy.

sentences.zh: 周人之思邵公，愛其甘棠，又況其子孫哉！

translations.literal: The Zhou people's longing for Duke Shao, loving his sweet pear—how much more his descendants!
translations.idiomatic: The men of Zhou still cherish Lord Shao for the pear tree he left them—how much more do men cherish his line?

sentences.zh: 劉氏之再受命，蓋以此乎！

translations.literal: The Liu clan's receiving the mandate again—was it by this!
translations.idiomatic: Perhaps that is why the Liu house received Heaven’s mandate a second time.

sentences.zh: 若數子者，豈有國之遠圖哉！

translations.literal: As for these several men, how could they have a state's far-reaching plan!
translations.idiomatic: Men such as these had no long design for the state.

sentences.zh: 因時擾攘，茍瓷縱而已耳，然猶以附假宗室，能掘強歲月之間。

translations.literal: Riding the times' turmoil, they were but recklessly self-indulgent; yet still by attaching and borrowing the imperial clan, were able to be stubborn strong for a space of years and months.
translations.idiomatic: They fed on turmoil and lived by opportunism and brute force, yet by borrowing the Liu name they defied the throne for years.

sentences.zh: 觀其智略，固無足以憚漢祖，發其英靈者也。

translations.literal: Observing their wisdom and strategy, indeed there was nothing sufficient to make Han's ancestor fear, or rouse his heroic spirit.
translations.idiomatic: Measured against true statecraft, none of them could have given the founder of Han a moment’s unease.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：天地閉革，野戰群龍。

translations.literal: The eulogy says: Heaven and earth closed and changed; in the field battled many dragons.
translations.idiomatic: The summation runs: When heaven and earth closed in revolution, dragons fought in the wilds.

sentences.zh: 昌、芳僭詐，梁、齊連鋒。

translations.literal: Chang and Fang usurped and deceived; Liang and Qi joined spears.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Chang and Lu Fang ruled by fraud; Liang and Qi joined arms.

sentences.zh: 寵負強地，憲縈深江。

translations.literal: Chong relied on strong territory; Xian wound about the deep river.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Chong leaned on a strong province; Li Xian tangled with Han among the deep rivers.

sentences.zh: 實惟非律，代委神邦。

translations.literal: Truly it was not according to law; the substitute entrusted the divine state.
translations.idiomatic: They were outlaws who usurped the sacred charge of the realm.
